𝗢𝗗𝗜𝗙𝗙 | Visual regression testing and identifying pixel-level differences between images, especially in automated testing pipelines.  🔥 Super Hot!

•••••••••••••••••••••••

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💻 Zig 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Visual regression testing and identifying pixel-level differences between images, especially in automated testing pipelines.
📅 Created: Nov 2020 • Updated: Apr 2026

•••••••••••••••••••••••

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ SIMD-first image comparison
✅ Support for PNG, JPEG, JPG, WebP, TIFF
✅ Cross-format comparison
✅ Handles images with different layouts
✅ Anti-aliasing detection
✅ Ignoring regions
✅ YIQ NTSC algorithm for visual difference
✅ SSE2, AVX2, AVX512, NEON optimizations
✅ Controlled memory footprint
✅ 100% test coverage
✅ Command-line interface
✅ Node.js bindings
✅ Server mode for Node.js


•••••••••••••••••••••••

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2.9K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 101 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 13 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

•••••••••••••••••••••••

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Odiff is a high-performance image comparison library written in Zig, optimized with SIMD instructions for speed. It provides pixel-by-pixel difference detection for various image formats and offers both a command-line interface and Node.js bindings for integration into testing workflows.

•••••••••••••••••••••••

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/dmtrKovalenko/odiff

•••••••••••••••••••••••

•••••••••••••••••••••••


🏷️ 𝗧𝗔𝗚𝗦
#diff #hacktoberfest #image_comparison #odiff #pixel_perfect

𝗖𝗢𝗗𝗘𝗙𝗢𝗥𝗠𝗘𝗥 | Restoring and enhancing the quality of degraded or low-resolution facial images.  ✨ Notable!

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Restoring and enhancing the quality of degraded or low-resolution facial images.
📅 Created: Jun 2022 • Updated: Apr 2026

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Robust blind face restoration
✅ Codebook Lookup Transformer architecture
✅ Face inpainting
✅ Face color enhancement and restoration
✅ Support for video input
✅ Integration with Hugging Face, Replicate, and OpenXLab for demos


◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 17.9K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 3.7K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 262 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

CodeFormer is a PyTorch-based framework for robust blind face restoration, utilizing a Codebook Lookup Transformer. It excels at enhancing degraded facial images, fixing AI-generated art, and can also perform face inpainting and colorization.

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/sczhou/CodeFormer

◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇◇

𝗧𝗘𝗚𝗔𝗞𝗜 | Creating animated handwriting effects for text on the web, enhancing user interfaces with a dynamic and personalized touch.  🔥 Super Hot!

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Creating animated handwriting effects for text on the web, enhancing user interfaces with a dynamic and personalized touch.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: Apr 2026

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Animates any font into handwriting
✅ No manual path authoring required
✅ No native dependencies
✅ Supports React, Svelte, Vue, SolidJS, Astro, Web Components, and Vanilla JS
✅ Bundled handwriting fonts available
✅ Interactive generator for custom font bundles


⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 2.1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 72 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 5 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

Tegaki is a TypeScript library that animates any font to appear as if it's being written by hand. It requires no manual path authoring and supports integration with major web frameworks like React, Svelte, Vue, and SolidJS, as well as Vanilla JS and Web Components.

⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯⎯

• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/KurtGokhan/tegaki

𝗟𝗟𝗠_𝗪𝗜𝗞𝗜_𝗔𝗚𝗘𝗡𝗧 | To automatically build and maintain a personal, interlinked knowledge base (wiki) from diverse raw data sources, powered by an LLM.  ✨ Notable!

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🤖 Kotlin 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To automatically build and maintain a personal, interlinked knowledge base (wiki) from diverse raw data sources, powered by an LLM.
📅 Created: Apr 2026 • Updated: Apr 2026

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Ingests raw sources (URLs, PDFs, audio, video)
✅ Extracts content using Apache Tika
✅ Transcribes audio/video using OpenAI Whisper
✅ Generates interlinked wiki pages compatible with Obsidian
✅ Manages wiki content (CRUD, search, index, log)
✅ Communicates with LLM via MCP protocol (STDIO)
✅ Built as an MCP server in Kotlin + Spring Boot


▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 16 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 4 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

The llm_wiki_agent is a Kotlin-based server that acts as a toolkit for an LLM (Claude Code) to ingest various raw data sources like URLs, PDFs, and audio/video files. It extracts and processes this content, transforming it into an interlinked Obsidian-compatible wiki, enabling a personal knowledge management system.

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/Anton15K/llm_wiki_agent

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗧𝗘𝗟𝗘𝗚𝗥𝗔𝗠_𝗘𝗫 | Developing Telegram bots using the Elixir programming language, with support for both simple and complex state management.  🔥 Super Hot!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📊 Elixir 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Developing Telegram bots using the Elixir programming language, with support for both simple and complex state management.
📅 Created: Mar 2026 • Updated: Apr 2026

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Macro-based API for bot development
✅ Handles messages, callbacks, and inline queries
✅ Supports automatic polling
✅ Stateless handler implementation
✅ Stateful handler implementation with FSM
✅ Integration into Elixir supervision trees
✅ Configuration via `config/runtime.exs`


━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐ 31 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 2 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 0 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

TelegramEx is an Elixir library designed for building Telegram bots. It offers a macro-based API for simplified handling of messages, callbacks, and inline queries, supporting both stateless and stateful (FSM) bot logic.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
